title: Kalman/state-space latent degradation features instead of raw degradation alone
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit stops as structural resets, producing interpretable degradation rates and time-varying state estimates.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit stops as structural resets, producing interpretable degradation rates and time-varying state estimates. Your current features use observed degradation and rolling deltas, but not an inferred hidden pace/degradation state.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint, fit a lightweight local-linear/Kalman-style filter on lap time, lap delta, tyre life, and cumulative degradation to estimate latent pace, degradation slope, slope acceleration, and one-step innovation residuals. Feed those latent summaries into the existing tree model rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Can denoise noisy lap-level observables and expose the ‘tyres are going off now’ transition more cleanly than raw rolling means.
risk: If the filter is too flexible it can memorize session idiosyncrasies; keep parameterization simple and estimate any global hyperparameters on training folds only.
